steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the dropdown under the "New Project" dialog.
Click the "No Action" entry in the dropdown menu under the "Add an action to your declarative agent" header, located within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to set the project root folder path (`/home/.vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`).
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel is active.
Type 'Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Press Enter to select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts" command from the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Sign out of Microsoft 365: ${{env: M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette dropdown.
Click the "Sign out" button in the sign-out confirmation dialog for "${{env: M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" in the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion there is an item "Sign in to Microsoft 365" in ACCOUNTS section
Click the "Accounts" icon in the bottom left status bar of Visual Studio Code, opening the account management and sign-in options.
Click on the "${{env: AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" account entry in the context menu under the ACCOUNTS section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Sign Out" option in the context menu for the Microsoft 365 account (${{env: AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}) within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Sign Out" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sign-out confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there is an item "Sign in to Azure" in ACCOUNTS section
Click the "Sign in to Microsoft 365" button in the ACCOUNTS section of the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit popup within Visual Studio Code to initiate authentication for a Microsoft 365 developer sandbox.
Click the "${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" account option in the Microsoft login "Pick an account" prompt in Google Chrome.
Click the "Password" input field on the Microsoft sign-in page in Google Chrome to focus the cursor for password entry.
Type '${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}' into the password field on the Microsoft login page for user “${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}” in Google Chrome.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page to submit the entered password for the account test31@xxbdw.onmicrosoft.com.
Click the close ("X") button on the "M365 Account - Sign In" browser tab in Google Chrome to close the authentication tab after successful sign-in.
@assertion there is ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} in ACCOUNTS section
Click the "${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" Microsoft 365 account entry in the ACCOUNTS section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar within Visual Studio Code.
Click the gear icon next to the "${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" account in the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" sidebar of Visual Studio Code to open the account options menu.
Click the "Sign out" button on the account sign-out confirmation dialog for 'test31@xxbdw.onmicrosoft.com' in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there is "Sign in to Microsoft 365" in ACCOUNTS section
